description: Function-as-a-service compute and backend-as-a-service platforms for event-driven application logic.

entries.name: AWS Lambda
entries.summary: Pioneer of serverless functions; runs code in response to events without managing servers.
entries.freeTier: 1M requests/month and 400,000 GB-seconds compute free, always-free (not time-limited).

entries.name: Google Cloud Functions
entries.summary: Event-driven serverless functions integrated with the Google Cloud and Firebase ecosystems.

entries.name: Azure Functions
entries.summary: Microsoft's serverless compute service, integrates tightly with the .NET ecosystem and Azure services.
entries.paidFrom: Consumption plan billed per execution beyond free tier.

entries.name: Cloudflare Workers
entries.summary: V8-isolate-based edge functions with extremely low cold-start latency, running in 300+ global locations.

entries.name: Vercel Functions
entries.summary: Serverless and edge functions deeply integrated with Next.js and the Vercel deployment pipeline.
entries.freeTier: Included in the free Hobby plan with limits on execution time/invocations.
entries.paidFrom: Pro plan from $20/month for higher limits.

entries.name: Deno Deploy
entries.summary: Globally distributed serverless JavaScript/TypeScript runtime built on Deno, with zero-config deploys.
entries.freeTier: Free tier with generous request and CPU-time allowances for hobby projects.
entries.paidFrom: Pro plan billed per usage beyond free tier.

entries.name: Appwrite
entries.summary: Open-source backend-as-a-service with auth, databases, storage, and functions, self-hostable or managed cloud.
entries.freeTier: Self-hosted fully free; Cloud free tier includes generous bandwidth/storage/function execution.

entries.name: Convex
entries.summary: Reactive backend platform combining database, server functions, and real-time sync into one TypeScript-first system.
entries.freeTier: Free tier with generous function calls and database bandwidth for small projects.
